V1.name: Fundamentals Trajectory
V1.rationale: EBIT margin trajectory now **5 consecutive periods** delivered THROUGH macro shock: 4.9% → 5.1% → 6.8% → 7.2% ex-FX → **7.8% (FY26 pre-close, 29 Apr 2026)** above the 6.7% re-entry gate. Pricing-power empirically validated (margin held with Brent $96-99 + Hormuz impaired + Saudi disruption + £8M Malaysia closure drag). Bufab Q1 2026 record gross 32.9% / adj-EBITDA 15.3% corroborates fastener-distribution sector pricing/mix discipline. Revenue declining (FY26 £207M, -7% YoY, £7M below consensus) but deliberate — low-margin customer exits + Malaysia closure transition. "Strongest commercial pipeline in two years" + reaffirmed >10% medium-term EBIT target. ACEA Q1 +4% YoY (Mar +12.5% six-year high) supports H2 FY27 demand. **Management alignment sub-assessment:** (1) Structural — leverage 0.9x (well below 3.0x covenant); £73.4M undrawn; IC ladder 3.25 → 3.50 → 4.00x effective 31 Dec 2026; Mills bloc 17.41% de-facto governance overlay. (2) Signals — ~£1.03M insider buying across 5 insiders incl. Mills £896K @ 59.75p Mar absorbing Slater 207bps distribution same-day; Percival buying *below* his Feb cost basis; no insider selling. **Not EXTREME:** revenue in deliberate decline (not "all three accelerating"); 220bps margin gap to 10% target remains; pipeline language qualitative not quantified backlog. **Not AVERAGE:** prior "easy wins done" debate hypothesis materially refuted by margin held through shock. **DATA GAPs:** auto OEM/aftermarket split + mfg/dist margin differential remain open (could compress V1 to AVERAGE if aftermarket <20%).

V2.name: Valuation Mismatch
V2.rationale: **PW-FV 129p vs current 63.40p = +103% mismatch** (revised 2026-04-30 from 120p/+71% on prob shift Bull 25→30 / Base 45→50 / Stall 20→15 / Recession 10→5; price drop 70p → 63.40p widened mismatch). Multi-method stress test: PW-FV +103%; generalist FV (thesis-independent peer-median) 121p +91%; peer half-gap close 127p +100%; M&A EV/Sales midpoint 130p +105%; M&A premium-based midpoint 118p +86%; FCF yield ~17.5% on FY26E £19M. ALL non-bear methods cluster +86-105% (>50% STRONG threshold). Bossard ONWC 46.7% reconciliation invalidates the "TRI WC bloated" sub-anchor in the bear narrative. Bufab Q1 record margins prove margin-walk runway is real (not just TRI accounting). Diagnostic reference: market-vs-me-diagnostic 2026-04-21 (~9 days, within 30-day window) verdict aligns with "market likely wrong." **Not EXTREME:** recession bear modelled FV 50p < current 63.40p (-21%) — fails "floor-case FV > current across every reasonable parameterization" anchor. **Not AVERAGE:** every non-floor method >85% mismatch; consensus PTs are market-positioning data per Foundational Principle 1, not V2 input.

V3.name: Identifiable Mispricing Reason
V3.rationale: 4|—|Five reasons, all temporary: (1) £81M micro-cap limits some mandates despite **FTSE Small Cap / All-Share membership** — passive index funds must hold, partially answering "incremental buyer" question. (2) Coverage gap: 3 analysts but Peel Hunt sole broker — Zeus/Panmure NOT confirmed as covering (bookrunner claim retracted). Active distribution mechanism narrow. (3) 81% drawdown narrative anchoring. (4) Auto narrative anchoring at 35% while smart infra growing to 18%. (5) FY2024 statutory loss screening distortion. FTSE membership is the strongest positive finding since original scoring — it proves a passive buying mechanism exists. Not 5 — no hard forcing function with known date. Closest: FY2026 results Jul 2026.

V4.name: Downside Protection
V4.rationale: **Type B — Balance Sheet as Torque** (covenant gates + leverage). Trades at ~0.71x book value (£122.7M book vs ~£86M mcap at 63.40p). Leverage 0.9x vs 3.0x covenant; £73.4M undrawn. **Covenant math:** IC schedule 3.25 → 3.50x (effective 31 Dec 2025–30 Sep 2026) → **4.00x effective 31 Dec 2026**. At current low drawings (£2-3M finance cost), FY26 EBIT ~£16M = ~83% IC headroom on 3.50x. Stress (£30-40M acquisition + EBIT 7%): finance cost £4.5M → minimum EBIT £15.75M = marginal (FY25 was £15.6M); but 29 Apr pre-close 7.8% margin proof materially de-risks. **Pre-mortem mode updates 2026-04-30:** auto recession DE-RISKED (ACEA Q1 +4% YoY; Bufab record margins); margin-stall hypothesis MATERIALLY WEAKENED (5th consecutive margin period through shock); oil-driven inflation FADING (Brent $96-99 vs $124 peak); banking renewal RESOLVED (£70M RCF Jul 2027/2028 + £50M UKEF EDG 2028); Saudi REFRAMED (named drag, force-majeure not invoked, bounded in £8M FY27 envelope). **Persistent concerns:** council "asset-floor illusion" stands (inventory £74.2M + receivables £52.5M of £232M total); transient overshoot 25-35p possible in compound-failure thin liquidity; auto OEM/aftermarket split + inventory reserve methodology + Saudi capex quantum still UNRESOLVED. **Floor analysis:** tangible book ~74p > current 63.40p (+17% cushion); M&A floor 82-118p > current; Slater/Mills/Ruffer ~34% concert-party-adjacent register would resist takeout below tangible book. Recession bear modelled 50p < current (-21%, 5% prob). **Not STRONG:** STRONG anchor requires "few identifiable failure modes, all bounded" — 7+ failure modes remain (acquisition misstep, cyberattack, FTSE relegation, UK CGU impairment, transient overshoot, Saudi capex, WC-heavy floor quality); compound-failure 25-35p transient is not "modest and recoverable." **Not WEAK:** solvency risk genuinely low; Primary Bear (stall, 70p) now ABOVE current (+10%) — protected floor on most-likely bear path.

V5.name: Thesis Simplicity
V5.rationale: 4|—|Two conditions, both currently **HOLDING**: (1) Margin improvement continues via self-help — 4 consecutive periods confirmed (4.9%→5.1%→6.7%→7.2% ex-FX). Under increased pressure from freight/oil but no reversal yet. **BREACH TRIGGER: FY2026 full-year EBIT margin <6.5% (i.e. below FY2025's 6.7%).** (2) Revenue stays above ~£190M — FY26E £214M consensus, substantial headroom. Revenue growth NOT required. Auto recovery NOT required. M&A NOT required. Not 5 — genuinely two conditions, and Condition 1 is under more near-term pressure than at inception.

V6.name: Upside Optionality
V6.rationale: 5|—|Seven independent paths, at least 3 showing early evidence: (1) Margin reaches 10%+ — FY2019 achieved 11.6%, proving it's possible. (2) Analyst coverage initiation — uncertain (Zeus/Panmure not confirmed); Peel Hunt sole broker. (3) M&A takeout by Bossard/Bufab/PE — TRI at 0.53x EV/Sales vs industry M&A at 0.6-1.2x; sub-£100M EV for global platform. (4) Smart infra grows to 30% of revenue — currently 18%, growing at 11.5%. (5) NA bolt-on acquisition — active pipeline, balance sheet supports. (6) Saudi Arabia expansion — planned launch OVERDUE (Q3 FY2026), status unknown; GCC data center investment NOT cancelled ($13.5B+ pipeline); conditional optionality with modest downside. (7) FTSE Small Cap weighting increases as market cap grows → reflexive passive buying. Still 5 — even with Saudi uncertainty, 6 other paths remain viable. Multiple independent catalysts with early evidence on at least 3.

V7.name: Evidence Quality
V7.rationale: OVERVIEW.md sourced from audited FY2024 AR (236pp); FY2025 AR data integrated; H1 FY2026 interim (Sep 2025); 7+ transcripts reviewed; Q3 Trading Statement; **29 Apr 2026 pre-close trading statement** (NEW). Banking facility fully resolved + IC covenant schedule confirmed. Insider purchases tracked across 5 insiders (~£1.03M cumulative, RNS-sourced). Mills bloc 17.41% TR-1 disclosed; Slater 10.98%→8.91% disclosed. **Council 5-gap status (refreshed 2026-04-30):** (1) Auto aftermarket vs new-production split — STILL OPEN (industry benchmark 92.56% OEM / 7.44% aftermarket inferred; TRI-specific undisclosed); (2) Interest cover EBIT-breach floor — PARTIALLY RESOLVED (covenant schedule confirmed; EBIT floor inferred ~£15.75M at 3.50x); (3) Saudi facility capex/status — PARTIALLY RESOLVED (29 Apr disruption named, force-majeure NOT invoked, drag bounded in £8M FY27 envelope; capex quantum still undisclosed); (4) Manufactured vs distributed margin differential — STILL OPEN; (5) Inventory reserve methodology — STILL OPEN. **Quantitative read: 1.5 of 5 gaps closed; 3 of 5 remain OPEN with thesis-relevant impact.** **Not STRONG:** STRONG anchor requires "no critical DATA GAPs"; three remain. **Not WEAK:** primary evidence base is deep and fact-checked; FY26 pre-close added incremental fact-base; 7 transcripts reviewed cumulatively. **Path to STRONG:** Jul 2026 audited finals + Saudi capex disclosure + auto OEM/aftermarket Q&A. **V7 = AVERAGE triggers no caps on V1/V2/V4** per §10 gating rule.

V8.name: Catalyst Clarity
V8.rationale: 4|—|Hard-dated: Late April 2026 pre-close statement (~1 month — first RNS post-Iran conflict, will address Saudi/freight/FY26 outlook). FY2026 Final Results Jul 2026 (~4 months — decisive for margin trajectory). H1 FY2027 Nov 2026 (~8 months). Semi-annual reporting = natural forcing function. Soft-dated: analyst initiation (uncertain — Peel Hunt sole broker limits distribution); M&A (speculative but valuation supports); Saudi operational confirmation (overdue). Not 5 — highest-impact catalyst (coverage initiation) lacks specific date and the active distribution mechanism is narrower than previously assumed.

blindspots: **Chinese fastener import competition pricing** — Chinese fasteners are a low-cost threat, particularly in non-critical applications. TRI's moat is in certified/safety-critical fasteners (automotive, aerospace, medical). But if Chinese competitors begin achieving certifications, the moat narrows. No reliable tracking source for Chinese fastener certification progress in European/UK markets.

**M&A interest from Bossard/Bufab/PE** — At sub-£100M EV, TRI is an attractive acquisition target for larger fastener distributors or PE. But there's no public signal until a formal approach or leak. Proxy: insider buying patterns (management may resist selling if they see value; or may sell if approach is attractive).

**Medical/smart infrastructure pipeline size** — TRI is growing these segments but provides limited quantitative disclosure on pipeline or order book. Success here validates the diversification thesis away from auto. Monitor via: management commentary in interim reports.

**Automotive EV fastener content per vehicle** — Key bull thesis element: EVs need MORE fasteners per vehicle than ICE (battery pack, thermal management, structural). But quantification is sparse. Industry estimates range from 1.0x to 1.5x ICE content. If closer to 1.0x, the "EV transition is positive for fasteners" argument weakens.

noiseFilter: **UK small-cap sentiment** — Structural outflows from UK small-caps affect broadly. TRI is LSE Main Market (FTSE Small Cap constituent) — passive inclusion provides baseline demand, but discretionary small-cap flows still matter.
**GBP/EUR moves <5%** — TRI has natural hedging from EUR-denominated costs. Small FX moves don't affect economics; only translation of reported numbers.

**Single-quarter revenue miss if margin trend intact** — The thesis is margin recovery, not revenue growth. A revenue miss with improving margins is consistent with the Rebuild strategy (exiting low-margin business).

**Peer share price moves (Bossard, Bufab)** — These are much larger, more liquid, better-covered companies. Their share price dynamics are driven by different factors (institutional flows, index inclusion). Not informative for TRI.

Board FY expectations unchanged, 10% target maintained. No new data in 2026-04-13–20 window; next disclosure FY26 YE trading update (expected Apr/May 2026).
